'''Brolsam''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] within the [[Brolsam system/Legends|system of the same name]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. An [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]], it was located on a branch off the [[Trition Trade Route]], between the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] and [[Torize]]. Although its distance from the sector's major [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade route]] hampered its economic growth, Brolsam developed an industry based primarily around [[Farm/Legends|agriculture]] due to the presence of a significant community of [[Fefze]] swarms. The planet also gained a reputation as an [[Artist/Legends|artist's]] retreat. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the planet was on the brink of civil war following the disintegration of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] authority in the sector after the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] of Kal'Shebbol by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]].

==Description==
The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Brolsam was located in the [[Brolsam system/Legends|Brolsam system]],<ref name="DarkStryder note" /> which was part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]' [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. The world was reached by traveling along a spur off the [[Trition Trade Route]] situated between the [[Kal'Shebbol system/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] and [[Torize system/Legends|Torize systems]]. Brolsam was connected to the [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] and [[Aaris system/Legends|Aaris systems]] by [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]; the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of Kal'Shebbol was one [[Standard day/Legends|day]] and six [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]]' travel from Brolsam by Class One [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], while [[Aaris III]] was three days and nine hours distant. The planet lacked important resources, and its distance from the Trition Trade Route hampered its development and population growth. The planet developed as an [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural world]], with extensive fields, although there was areas on the surface that contained back alleys. The planet was home to fifteen million inhabitants, divided equally between [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], and [[Fefze]].<ref name="TDC" />

==History==
When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] took control of the Kathol sector, Brolsam was brought under Imperial control. Imperial officials were installed to rule over the population, and the new administration was secretly supported by the [[Nat'xikta]]—a small but wealthy Fefze tribe that engaged in [[Spy/Legends|spy]] operations for the Empire.<ref name="O">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Omens|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> When the ruler of the sector, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], broke from the Empire following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Brolsam became part of his [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] holdings.<ref name="TDC" />

[[File:Tritiontraderoute.jpg|thumb|left|The Trition Trade Route, with Brolsam located on a spur between Kal'Shebbol and Torize]]
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' takes place four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which equates to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> following the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] of Kal'Shebbol by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and their subsequent increase in influence in the sector,<ref name="TSB">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> many Fefze swarms allied themselves with disaffected Humans and Rodians and began rounding up and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoning]] Imperial officials and sympathizers. Their efforts were disorganized, however, and Imperial forces resorted to guerrilla tactics to keep them off-balance. The situation threatened to plunge the entire planet into civil war. The Nat'xikta tribe—fearful of losing their status and power on the planet—began plotting to liberate the imprisoned Imperials. They hired Rodian [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], and were prepared to [[Death/Legends|sacrifice]] hundreds of their own swarm to ensure success.<ref name="O" />

{{RPGseed}}
The New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' visited the planet<ref name="O" /> sometime during its [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to locate Moff Sarne, who had fled Kal'Shebbol when it was assaulted by the New Republic.<ref name="TSB" /> A [[mission group]] was deployed to the planet, and was subsequently attacked by Rodian mercenaries. During a brief battle, one of the members of the mission group was captured. [[Executive officer/Legends|First Officer]] [[Gorak Khzam]] and procurement specialist [[Loh'khar]] the Finder used their knowledge of the planet, along with their own skills and contacts, to locate and rescue their missing crewmate. They also discovered the Nat'xikta swarm's plans concerning the imprisoned Imperials.<ref name="O" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
Although Brolsam's population included sizable Human and Rodian communities, it was the Fefze swarms that shaped much of the planet's culture. The swarms were vital to the development of the planet's [[Farm/Legends|agricultural]] industry, and their presence fostered a ''genteel [[Artist/Legends|artiste]]'' community centered around their love of art festivals, sculptures and [[Music/Legends|song]].<ref name="O" />

==Behind the scenes==
Brolsam was featured in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]].<ref name="TDC" /> Although not featured directly in any adventure, the planet was included so that Gamemasters could use the location in an original adventure. A specific "adventure hook" was detailed in the scenario ''[[Omens]]''. This article assumes that the ''FarStar'' did indeed travel to the planet as detailed in the published adventure hook.<ref name="O" />
